_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Maintain knowledge of applicable cyber defense policies, regulations, and compliance documents specifically related to cyber defense auditing.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of regulatory examination and audit processes, including common examination scopes, documentation expectations, evidence standards, examiner interaction protocols, and typical findings for data and AI regulatory reviews.
comments: DCWF task superset of WIDAI RC domain. RC-K-014 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DG-K-008 (DG, cosine: 0.543), SP-K-006 (SP, cosine: 0.538).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.2/10 (raw 0.622). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge]. DCWF-only terms [applicable, auditing, compliance, cyber, defense, documents] vs WIDAI-only terms [ai, audit, common, data, documentation, evidence] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.877 (P=0.887, R=0.868). Balanced P/R — the 17-word DCWF element and 27-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (71.3%). Despite 'Superset of' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Maintain knowledge of applicable cyber defense policies, regulations, and compli" and "Knowledge of regulatory examination and audit processes, including common examin". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.567 < STS 0.622 (gap -0.055). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [applicable, auditing, compliance] and [ai, audit, common] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 8.6%. Shared tokens: [knowledge]. DCWF-only: [applicable, auditing, compliance, cyber, defense, documents]. WIDAI-only: [ai, audit, common, data, documentation, evidence].
